behaviors.description: Code review analysis is restricted to changed lines in the diff, not the entire file content
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is that the agent analyzes the full file instead of only the changed lines. To catch this, multiple modules must coordinate: diff parsing → filtering → analysis. A unit test of just the diff parser wouldn't catch if the analyzer ignores the filtering and processes all 200 lines anyway. Testing requires running the full pipeline with a realistic diff (e.g., changes only in lines 50-70) and a file containing issues at other lines (lines 1-10, 190-200) to verify they're excluded. This coordination failure only surfaces at Integration level.
behaviors.test_description: Create a pull request diff with changes only in lines 50-70 of a 200-line file. Seed the full file with intentional issues at lines 5, 60, and 195. Run the analysis and verify the findings list only includes issues in the changed range (lines 50-70), excluding issues outside the diff.

behaviors.description: Inline review comments are posted to the correct line numbers in GitHub's diff view
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is comments posted at incorrect line numbers in GitHub's API calls. Integration level (mocked GitHub API with realistic diff data) can catch off-by-one errors and incorrect line coordinate mappings. System level would be needed if the failure depends on GitHub's actual diff format complexity (e.g., only certain lines are commentable, line numbering differs between file and diff view). Integration testing with a mock can verify the correct parameters are sent; System testing (real GitHub) would catch format or API behavior surprises.
behaviors.test_description: Create a diff showing changes on specific file line numbers (e.g., lines 42, 44, 46). Mock the GitHub API to capture all outgoing comment requests. Run the agent and verify each inline comment request contains the correct line number or diff position mapping. Cross-check that line numbers in the mock calls correspond to the actual changed lines.

behaviors.description: The agent successfully detects at least one legitimate defect in code containing a known introduced bug
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is that the LLM fails to identify a known bug in the code. No mock or deterministic test can simulate the LLM's analysis capability. The outcome depends on real LLM non-determinism—running the test 100 times may yield different results (sometimes the bug is caught, sometimes not). To verify the agent can identify defects, a real LLM is required. This is purely an Agentic concern; GitHub API availability or code delivery mechanism doesn't determine defect detection, the LLM's reasoning does.
behaviors.test_description: Provide the agent with a pull request containing a known, intentional bug (e.g., an uncaught exception path, off-by-one loop error, or missing null check). Run the agent with a real LLM model. Verify that the findings list includes a comment identifying the bug. Repeat multiple times to account for LLM variance.

behaviors.description: All findings included in the summary PR comment have corresponding inline comments, with no extra or missing items
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is divergence between inline comments and summary: a finding appears in the summary without a matching inline comment, or vice versa. This is a coordination failure between two separate outputs. A unit test of just the summary generation wouldn't catch this—the outputs might be generated from different code paths that fall out of sync. Testing requires running both operations together and verifying the set of findings is identical. Mocking the GitHub API (Integration) is sufficient to capture both outgoing API calls and compare their payloads.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API to record all outgoing comment requests (both inline and summary). Run the agent. Extract the set of findings from each inline comment and the summary comment. Verify that the findings sets are identical: every inline comment issue appears in the summary, and the summary contains no extra findings not covered by inline comments.

behaviors.description: The complete review workflow (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) executes successfully on a real PR without errors or manual intervention
behaviors.justification: The failure mode includes infrastructure-level issues that Integration testing with mocks cannot catch: GitHub API authentication errors, rate limiting, network failures, repository permission issues, or unexpected edge cases in real PR structure (merge commits, large diffs, binary files, etc.). The phrase 'real pull request' in the behavior signals System-level scope. Integration testing can verify the orchestration logic with mocks, but only System level (actual GitHub interaction) can catch failures that emerge from real API contracts, timing, or repository state.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test pull request on a real GitHub repository (or test GitHub instance). Invoke the agent end-to-end without any manual steps. Verify that the agent completes without errors, successfully posts at least one inline comment, and posts a summary comment. Check for proper error handling if the API returns transient errors. Verify no partial state (e.g., inline comments posted but summary missing).
